summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes XOR-encrypted Windows binary, then erases itself from the dependency tree
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall hook (`node install-hook.js`) runs a hidden PowerShell command via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>`. The decoded PowerShell downloads `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, launches it hidden via Start-Process, sleeps 3 seconds, and deletes the file. After triggering the dropper, install-hook.js writes a cleanup script to `$TEMP/cleanup_<ts>.js` and spawns it detached; the cleanup removes the `mailconfirmer` entry from the installer's `package.json` and `package-lock.json` and recursively deletes the package's own folder under `node_modules` — anti-forensics behavior that hides the dependency after a single install. The advertised library surface (`index.js` exporting `sendConfirmationEmail` and `verifyToken`) is a stub that only `console.log`s; the package has no functional purpose beyond delivering the dropper. The fetched binary lives on a third-party GitHub account (`Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note`) unrelated to the package's stated email-confirmation purpose, the XOR key is static anti-AV obfuscation, execution is hidden, and the package erases itself post-install — there is no legitimate interpretation of this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
